steps.canonical_text: Find out, if you can, what they took or were exposed to, how much, and when. Look for bottles, containers, or plants near them.
Call emergency services or a poison control line now. Follow their instructions, which depend on the substance.
Do not make them vomit and do not give salt water, milk, or charcoal unless emergency services tell you to.
If the substance is on the skin or in the eyes, rinse with running water for at least 15 minutes while removing contaminated clothing.
If they breathed in fumes, move them to fresh air right away. Open windows and stay safe yourself.
If they are unresponsive but breathing, place them on their side. If they stop breathing, start chest compressions.
Bring the container, label, or any sample of the substance to the hospital so the team can identify it.
